page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, providing 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with 16 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. Cache resources include 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate a distinct performance profile. The server exhibits strong single-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by a stress-ng single-core score of 2923.69, placing it in the top 25% of tested systems. However, multi-core scaling is a notable tradeoff; multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench falls into the bottom 25%. Memory performance is mixed, with large-block read and write bandwidth showing average results at 51.54 GB/sec and 38.79 GB/sec respectively, while small-block cached read bandwidth is relatively weak.

Given its strong single-core capabilities but weaker multi-threaded scaling, database operations, and web serving throughput, this server is best suited for workloads that prioritize single-threaded execution or moderate general-purpose tasks. These include lightweight single-threaded data processing, basic text processing, and small-scale LLM text generation using compact models. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated AMD EPYC hardware provides a stable compute baseline, though the resource density is optimized for workloads that do not require high multi-threaded parallelism or local NVMe storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 is a general-purpose, x86_64 architecture server configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 2 physical cores with hyper-threading, 32 MB of L3 cache, and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks reveal a strong single-core CPU capability, contrasted by weaker multi-core scaling and database throughput. Large-block memory bandwidth and single-threaded compression tasks perform at average levels. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources with a balanced memory-to-core ratio, but where massive multi-threaded parallelism is not critical. It is best suited for general-purpose computing, single-threaded data processing, and lightweight text-processing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 is an x86_64 server with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16 GB RAM. It features strong single-core performance but exhibits weaker multi-core scaling.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-4 features 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and strong single-core performance for general workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC instance delivering strong single-core performance and a balanced memory profile for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16 GB RAM
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in this configuration
Average large-block memory read and write bandwidth capabilities
